description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a mantis shrimp, a marine crustacean known for its elongated, segmented body and prominent raptorial claws. The exoskeleton displays a mottled pattern of greenish-brown and beige hues, with a hard, glossy surface texture. The cephalothorax is robust, featuring two large, forward-facing claws and multiple walking legs beneath the body. The abdomen is elongated and tapers toward a fan-shaped tail, with visible segmentation along its length. Two long antennae extend from the head, and the eyes are set on short stalks, providing a wide field of vision. The overall form is streamlined, with intricate details on the limbs and carapace, suggesting a lifelike and naturalistic appearance.
description: A lifelike mantis shrimp with a segmented, greenish-brown exoskeleton, large raptorial claws, and long antennae. The body is elongated and detailed, with a hard, glossy surface.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the mantis shrimp, highlighting the raptorial claws, segmented abdomen, and long antennae.
description_view_1: Frontal view emphasizing the broad cephalothorax, forward-facing claws, and the symmetry of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the body, with clear visibility of the claws, legs, and tail fan.
description_view_3: Dorsal (top-down) view revealing the segmentation of the abdomen, arrangement of the legs, and the overall body outline.
